name: Chromatin and Histone Modulation Coverage Assessment for June 12, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Backfill assessment of the published June 2 chromatin and histone modulation baseline and the June 5 no-op surveillance session; no new external source search was run for this assessment.

summary: Coverage is adequate for the current low-confidence animal_signal public claim because the local map contains multiple model-organism and mammalian anchors connecting chromatin or histone modulation to lifespan, healthspan, senescence, frailty, or disease-model multimorbidity: KAT7 inactivation, two SIRT6 overexpression mouse studies, SRT1720, spermidine-linked histone hypoacetylation and autophagy, beta-hydroxybutyrate HDAC biology, H3K4/H3K27 modifier genetics in C. elegans, and a 2026 butyrate/tributyrin histone-acylation mouse disease-model result. Coverage is not strong because the evidence is heterogeneous, often genetic or transgenic, pleiotropic, and boundary-track-heavy; no direct human chromatin or histone intervention shows aging-relevant target engagement, biomarkers, function, disease modification, or survival; chronic tissue-specific safety is unresolved; and recent-review, human trial-watch, pre-baseline SIRT6 chromatin-target-engagement, and formal seminal-anchor triangulation remain incomplete. The June 5 surveillance pass found no material post-publication delta, so ordinary surveillance can continue.

evidence_categories.rationale: The assessed public map has no direct human chromatin or histone intervention trial with aging-relevant target engagement, biomarkers, function, disease modification, or survival. Human relevance is limited to KAT7 human progeroid-cell screening and spermidine human immune-cell survival context rather than treated-human chromatin evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This is the main reason the track remains below human_biomarker_signal.

evidence_categories.rationale: The bootstrap and no-op surveillance artifacts do not systematically map human observational, natural-history, biopsy, epigenetic-aging, chromatin-accessibility, histone-mark, diet, ketone, polyamine, or microbiome-metabolite cohorts for this track.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This does not block the current animal_signal claim, but future review could decide whether observational human chromatin or histone trajectories would add useful translation or safety context.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline contains the current animal case: KAT7 inactivation extended aged and progeroid mouse lifespan, SIRT6 overexpression extended lifespan in two mouse studies, SRT1720 improved mouse healthspan and mean lifespan, spermidine and beta-hydroxybutyrate extended model-organism lifespan with histone or HDAC links, H3K4/H3K27 modifier genetics regulated C. elegans lifespan, and tributyrin/butyrate delayed multimorbidity and extended lifespan in mitochondrial-deficiency mice.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal evidence is adequate for a low-confidence animal_signal baseline, not for human translation or broad LEV relevance.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage spans KAT7/H3K14 acetylation, SIRT6 chromatin-linked deacylase and energy-homeostasis biology, SIRT1-linked SRT1720 biology, spermidine-linked histone hypoacetylation and autophagy, beta-hydroxybutyrate HDAC-linked biology, H3K4me3 and H3K27me3 modifier genetics, and butyrate-linked histone acylation rescue in a mitochondrial-deficiency model.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Adequate mechanistic coverage does not isolate chromatin causality for every signal; several mechanisms overlap autophagy, nutrient sensing, mitochondrial dysfunction, microbiome metabolites, senescence, DNA repair, and NAD biology.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public outlook and evidence reviews preserve the main limiting boundaries: SIRT6 lifespan effects include sex/background and broad pathway caveats, SRT1720 is broad SIRT1/NAD biology with mean-versus-maximum lifespan boundaries, spermidine is strongly autophagy-linked, beta-hydroxybutyrate is broad metabolic HDAC biology, and the 2026 butyrate/tributyrin result is a mitochondrial-deficiency disease model rather than normal aging.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The map has good interpretive caveats, but no broad failed-program or adverse-effect synthesis for chromatin/histone interventions has been formally reconciled.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public outlook names unresolved cancer, developmental, fertility, immune, neurocognitive, and tissue-repair tradeoffs from chronic chromatin modulation, but the assessed source map does not provide enough long-term selective-modulation safety, dose-response, reversibility, or tissue-specificity evidence for human translation.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Safety and durability should remain high-priority evidence requirements before confidence or human relevance is strengthened.

evidence_categories.rationale: The June 5 surveillance session checked ClinicalTrials.gov for SIRT6, KAT7, SIRT1, SRT1720, spermidine, beta-hydroxybutyrate, butyrate, tributyrin, HDAC, chromatin, and epigenetic-age terms. Same-window hits were no-results, observational, oncology, ketogenic, obesity, or biological-aging context and did not supply direct result-bearing chromatin/histone intervention evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Continue watching REPROGRAM, POLYCAD, spermidine, ketone, HDAC, SIRT1/SIRT6, and chromatin-aging registries for posted results or chromatin/histone target-engagement measures.

evidence_categories.rationale: The backfill reviewed local bootstrap and surveillance artifacts. The June 5 pass found HDAC, epigenetic-age, spermidine, autophagy-epigenetic, and chromatin review context, but no formal recent-review or meta-analysis triangulation has been reconciled against the nine-source baseline.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future source-completeness pass should compare recent chromatin-aging, histone-modifier, HDAC, sirtuin, spermidine, ketone, butyrate, and epigenetic-therapy reviews against the current source map.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes plausible anchors for KAT7 inactivation, two SIRT6 mouse studies, SRT1720, spermidine, beta-hydroxybutyrate, H3K4me3 complex genetics, UTX-1/H3K27 demethylase genetics, and butyrate/tributyrin disease-model histone-acylation support. It is not strong because no formal must-include, review-cited, or citation-network audit is recorded.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Formal anchor auditing could move this category from adequate to strong and decide whether PMID 42135312 should be staged or explicitly deferred.

evidence_categories.rationale: The outlook, bootstrap notes, and interpretation review explicitly separate chromatin and histone modulation from autophagy, nutrient sensing, NAD/sirtuin biology, mitochondrial dysfunction, microbiome-derived metabolites, cellular senescence, DNA repair, oncology epigenetic therapy, and general epigenetic-age association records.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Boundary maintenance remains important because future source additions can easily belong partly or primarily to neighboring tracks.

search_log_summary: This backfill reviewed local project artifacts: the published Chromatin and Histone Modulation outlook, June 2 bootstrap session, published candidate bundle, source-fidelity and interpretation reviews, publication event, and June 5 no-op surveillance session with its PubMed, ClinicalTrials.gov, bioRxiv/medRxiv, correction/retraction, and web-search log. No new PubMed, registry, preprint, citation-network, or web source search was run for this assessment.
source_selection_notes: PMID 33408182 and PMID 34050173 are the strongest mammalian anchors because they connect KAT7 inactivation or SIRT6 overexpression to aged/progeroid mouse lifespan, frailty, or aging markers.
PMID 22367546 is included as an earlier SIRT6 lifespan anchor while preserving the male-specific and background-specific boundary.
PMID 24582957 adds small-molecule mammalian support through SRT1720, but remains broad SIRT1/NAD biology with maximum-lifespan boundaries.
PMID 19801973 and PMID 25127866 are chromatin/histone-linked model-organism and metabolite anchors with strong autophagy, nutrient-sensing, or metabolic overlap.
PMID 20555324 and PMID 21834846 anchor direct histone methylation-state lifespan genetics in C. elegans.
PMID 41826362 is current mammalian disease-model support that links tributyrin/butyrate to restored histone acylation and lifespan extension in mitochondrial-deficiency mice.

PMID 42135312 was noted in June 5 surveillance as a pre-baseline source-completeness lead for SIRT6 chromatin target engagement, but it is not in covered_source_ids because no public source or finding exists yet and it did not change the current public stage.

known_gaps.description: No result-bearing human intervention shows chromatin or histone target engagement tied to aging-relevant biomarkers, function, disease progression, survival, or acceptable short-term safety.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at animal_signal until a human intervention reports direct chromatin or histone pathway engagement in aging-relevant tissue.

known_gaps.description: The strongest mammalian signals are KAT7 gene targeting and SIRT6 overexpression, while small-molecule and metabolite evidence is broad, pleiotropic, and not validated as selective chromatin or histone modulation in humans.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require selective, dose-controlled, tissue-aware intervention data before treating the track as a coherent human-ready intervention class.

known_gaps.description: Chronic chromatin or histone modulation could plausibly alter cancer, developmental, fertility, immune, neurocognitive, repair, and cell-state programs; the current source map does not resolve those tradeoffs.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require explicit long-term safety, reversibility, tissue specificity, and off-target review before strengthening confidence or human relevance.

known_gaps.description: PMID 42135312 was surfaced during surveillance as a pre-baseline SIRT6 mouse liver chromatin-accessibility source, but it has not been staged or explicitly excluded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Consider a later source-completeness pass to decide whether this SIRT6 chromatin target-engagement record should be added as support or documented as context-only.

known_gaps.description: Spermidine, beta-hydroxybutyrate, butyrate/tributyrin, SRT1720, SIRT6, and KAT7 evidence overlaps autophagy, nutrient sensing, NAD biology, mitochondrial dysfunction, microbiome-metabolite restoration, senescence, and DNA repair.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future staged records explicit about whether the result tests chromatin/histone engagement directly or belongs mainly to an adjacent mechanism.

known_gaps.description: REPROGRAM, POLYCAD, spermidine, ketone, HDAC, SIRT1/SIRT6, chromatin, epigenetic-age, and adjacent biological-aging registry contexts remain no-results or wrong-scope in the assessed surveillance log.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use ordinary surveillance to recheck posted results, linked publications, and whether any trial reports direct chromatin or histone target engagement.

known_gaps.description: Recent reviews and meta-analyses around chromatin aging, histone acetylation and methylation, sirtuins, HDACs, spermidine, ketones, butyrate, and epigenetic therapies have not been formally reconciled against the nine-source baseline.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Use a future source-completeness pass to compare recent review literature with the current source list and document missing decision-relevant records.

next_coverage_action: Continue ordinary surveillance for Chromatin and Histone Modulation. On the next pass, recheck human chromatin or histone target-engagement studies, KAT7 and SIRT6 intervention programs, spermidine, butyrate or tributyrin, beta-hydroxybutyrate, HDAC, and SIRT1/SIRT6 programs for result-bearing older-adult or aging-relevant data; reserve coverage_repair for a later PMID 42135312, review-triangulation, trial-watch, safety, or formal seminal-anchor audit if no material field delta appears.
